
================================================================================
// FILE: addons/echo_legacy_importer/legacy_json_importer.gd
================================================================================
@tool
class_name LegacyJsonImporter
extends RefCounted

const TYPE_MAP := {
    "fire_modes": "FireModeDefinition",
    "weapons": "WeaponDefinition",
    "abilities": "AbilityDefinition",
    "status_effects": "StatusEffectDefinition",
    "equipment": "EquipmentDefinition",
    "items": "ItemDefinition",
    "units": "UnitDefinition",
    "enemies": "EnemyDefinition",
    "objectives": "ObjectiveDefinition",
    "missions": "MissionDefinition",
    "research": "ResearchDefinition",
    "dialogues": "DialogueDefinition",
    "campaign_events": "CampaignEventDefinition",
    "chapters": "ChapterDefinition",
    "facilities": "FacilityDefinition",
    "assets": "AssetManifestDefinition",
}

const SCRIPT_MAP := {
    "FireModeDefinition": "res://scripts/data/fire_mode_definition.gd",
    "WeaponDefinition": "res://scripts/data/weapon_definition.gd",
    "AbilityDefinition": "res://scripts/data/ability_definition.gd",
    "StatusEffectDefinition": "res://scripts/data/status_effect_definition.gd",
    "EquipmentDefinition": "res://scripts/data/equipment_definition.gd",
    "ItemDefinition": "res://scripts/data/item_definition.gd",
    "UnitDefinition": "res://scripts/data/unit_definition.gd",
    "EnemyDefinition": "res://scripts/data/enemy_definition.gd",
    "ObjectiveDefinition": "res://scripts/data/objective_definition.gd",
    "MissionDefinition": "res://scripts/data/mission_definition.gd",
    "ResearchDefinition": "res://scripts/data/research_definition.gd",
    "DialogueDefinition": "res://scripts/data/dialogue_definition.gd",
    "CampaignEventDefinition": "res://scripts/data/campaign_event_definition.gd",
    "ChapterDefinition": "res://scripts/data/chapter_definition.gd",
    "FacilityDefinition": "res://scripts/data/facility_definition.gd",
    "AssetManifestDefinition": "res://scripts/data/asset_manifest_definition.gd",
}

var report: Dictionary
var resources_by_id: Dictionary = {}
var source_by_id: Dictionary = {}
var output_paths: Dictionary = {}

func import_file(source_path: String, output_root: String) -> Dictionary:
    report = {"source": source_path, "output_root": output_root, "created": 0, "warnings": [], "errors": []}
    resources_by_id.clear()
    source_by_id.clear()
    output_paths.clear()
    var file := FileAccess.open(source_path, FileAccess.READ)
    if file == null:
        _error("Could not open source file: %s" % source_path)
        return _finish_report()
    var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(file.get_as_text())
    if not parsed is Dictionary:
        _error("Root JSON value must be an object.")
        return _finish_report()
    var raw_bundle: Dictionary = parsed.get("definitions", parsed)
    var bundle: Dictionary = {}
    for key in raw_bundle:
        bundle[str(key).to_snake_case()] = raw_bundle[key]
    _create_resources(bundle, output_root)
    _resolve_references()
    _save_resources()
    return _finish_report()

func _create_resources(bundle: Dictionary, output_root: String) -> void:
    for category in TYPE_MAP:
        var entries: Variant = bundle.get(category, [])
        if not entries is Array:
            _warning("Category '%s' is not an array and was skipped." % category)
            continue
        var type_name: String = TYPE_MAP[category]
        var script_path: String = SCRIPT_MAP[type_name]
        var script: Script = load(script_path)
        if script == null:
            _error("Missing resource script: %s" % script_path)
            continue
        for raw in entries:
            if not raw is Dictionary:
                _warning("Skipped non-object entry in '%s'." % category)
                continue
            var entry: Dictionary = {}
            for key in raw:
                entry[str(key).to_snake_case()] = raw[key]
            var definition_id := StringName(str(entry.get("id", "")).strip_edges())
            if definition_id == &"":
                _error("Skipped %s entry without an id." % category)
                continue
            if resources_by_id.has(definition_id):
                _error("Duplicate id '%s'." % definition_id)
                continue
            var resource: Resource = script.new()
            _copy_scalar_properties(resource, entry)
            resource.set("id", definition_id)
            if String(resource.get("display_name")).is_empty():
                resource.set("display_name", str(entry.get("name", definition_id)))
            resources_by_id[definition_id] = resource
            source_by_id[definition_id] = entry
            var directory := output_root.path_join(category)
            DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(directory))
            output_paths[definition_id] = directory.path_join(_safe_file_name(String(definition_id)) + ".tres")

func _copy_scalar_properties(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var property_names := {}
    for property in resource.get_property_list():
        property_names[property.name] = property
    for key in source:
        if not property_names.has(key):
            continue
        var property: Dictionary = property_names[key]
        var value: Variant = source[key]
        if int(property.type) == TYPE_OBJECT or value is Dictionary or value is Array:
            continue
        if int(property.type) == TYPE_COLOR and value is String:
            resource.set(key, Color.from_string(value, Color.WHITE))
        else:
            resource.set(key, value)
    _copy_known_collections(resource, source)

func _copy_known_collections(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    for key in [
        "conditions",
        "actions",
        "entry_conditions",
        "exit_conditions",
        "required_flags",
        "rewards",
        "consequence_rules",
        "success_flags",
        "failure_flags",
        "activation_flags",
        "escalation_rules",
        "effects",
        "completion_actions",
        "metadata",
        "material_overrides",
    ]:
        if source.get(key) is Dictionary and _has_property(resource, StringName(key)):
            resource.set(key, (source[key] as Dictionary).duplicate(true))
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "tags")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "special_behaviours")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "target_ids")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "prerequisites")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "deployment_tags")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "unlock_ids")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "required_sockets")
    _set_string_name_array(resource, source, "adjacency_tags")

func _set_string_name_array(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary, key: String) -> void:
    if not source.get(key) is Array or not _has_property(resource, StringName(key)):
        return
    var values: Array[StringName] = []
    for value in source[key]:
        values.append(StringName(str(value)))
    resource.set(key, values)

func _has_property(resource: Resource, property_name: StringName) -> bool:
    for property in resource.get_property_list():
        if StringName(property.name) == property_name:
            return true
    return false

func _resolve_references() -> void:
    for definition_id in resources_by_id:
        var resource: Resource = resources_by_id[definition_id]
        var source: Dictionary = source_by_id[definition_id]
        _resolve_external_paths(resource, source)
        if resource is WeaponDefinition:
            var fire_modes: Array[FireModeDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(source.get("fire_modes", []), "FireModeDefinition"):
                fire_modes.append(resolved as FireModeDefinition)
            resource.fire_modes = fire_modes
        elif resource is UnitDefinition:
            resource.starting_weapon = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("starting_weapon", source.get("weapon_id", "")),
                "WeaponDefinition"
            ) as WeaponDefinition
            var equipment: Array[EquipmentDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("starting_equipment", source.get("equipment_ids", [])),
                "EquipmentDefinition"
            ):
                equipment.append(resolved as EquipmentDefinition)
            resource.starting_equipment = equipment
            var abilities: Array[AbilityDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("abilities", source.get("ability_ids", [])),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ):
                abilities.append(resolved as AbilityDefinition)
            resource.abilities = abilities
        elif resource is EquipmentDefinition:
            var granted: Array[AbilityDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("granted_abilities", []),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ):
                granted.append(resolved as AbilityDefinition)
            resource.granted_abilities = granted
        elif resource is ItemDefinition:
            resource.tactical_ability = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("tactical_ability", ""),
                "AbilityDefinition"
            ) as AbilityDefinition
        elif resource is MissionDefinition:
            var objectives: Array[ObjectiveDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("objectives", []),
                "ObjectiveDefinition"
            ):
                objectives.append(resolved as ObjectiveDefinition)
            resource.objectives = objectives
            resource.briefing_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("briefing_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
            resource.debrief_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("debrief_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
        elif resource is DialogueDefinition:
            _populate_dialogue(resource, source)
        elif resource is CampaignEventDefinition:
            resource.dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition
        elif resource is ChapterDefinition:
            var mandatory: Array[MissionDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("mandatory_missions", []),
                "MissionDefinition"
            ):
                mandatory.append(resolved as MissionDefinition)
            resource.mandatory_missions = mandatory
            var optional: Array[MissionDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("optional_missions", []),
                "MissionDefinition"
            ):
                optional.append(resolved as MissionDefinition)
            resource.optional_missions = optional
            var campaign_events: Array[CampaignEventDefinition] = []
            for resolved in _resolve_resource_array(
                source.get("campaign_events", []),
                "CampaignEventDefinition"
            ):
                campaign_events.append(resolved as CampaignEventDefinition)
            resource.campaign_events = campaign_events
            resource.opening_dialogue = _resolve_resource(
                source.get("opening_dialogue", ""),
                "DialogueDefinition"
            ) as DialogueDefinition

func _resolve_external_paths(resource: Resource, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var path_properties := {
        "map_scene": "PackedScene",
        "wrapper_scene": "PackedScene",
        "model_scene": "PackedScene",
        "portrait": "Texture2D",
        "icon": "Texture2D",
    }
    for property_name in path_properties:
        if not source.has(property_name) or not _has_property(resource, StringName(property_name)):
            continue
        var path := str(source[property_name])
        if path.is_empty():
            continue
        if not path.begins_with("res://"):
            _warning("Resource path for '%s' must use res://: %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        if not ResourceLoader.exists(path):
            _warning("Missing resource path for '%s': %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        var loaded := ResourceLoader.load(path)
        if loaded == null:
            _warning("Could not load resource path for '%s': %s" % [property_name, path])
            continue
        resource.set(property_name, loaded)

func _populate_dialogue(dialogue: DialogueDefinition, source: Dictionary) -> void:
    var raw_nodes: Variant = source.get("nodes", [])
    if not raw_nodes is Array:
        _warning("Dialogue '%s' has a non-array nodes field." % dialogue.id)
        return
    var nodes: Array[DialogueNodeDefinition] = []
    for raw_node in raw_nodes:
        if not raw_node is Dictionary:
            continue
        var node_source := _snake_case_dictionary(raw_node)
        var node := DialogueNodeDefinition.new()
        node.id = StringName(str(node_source.get("id", "")))
        node.speaker_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("speaker_id", "")))
        node.text = str(node_source.get("text", ""))
        node.next_node_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("next_node_id", "")))
        node.timeout_choice_id = StringName(str(node_source.get("timeout_choice_id", "")))
        node.conditions = _dictionary_copy(node_source.get("conditions", {}))
        node.actions = _dictionary_copy(node_source.get("actions", {}))
        var tags: Array[StringName] = []
        for tag in node_source.get("tags", []):
            tags.append(StringName(str(tag)))
        node.tags = tags
        var choices: Array[DialogueChoiceDefinition] = []
        for raw_choice in node_source.get("choices", []):
            if not raw_choice is Dictionary:
                continue
            var choice_source := _snake_case_dictionary(raw_choice)
            var choice := DialogueChoiceDefinition.new()
            choice.id = StringName(str(choice_source.get("id", "")))
            choice.text = str(choice_source.get("text", ""))
            choice.next_node_id = StringName(str(choice_source.get("next_node_id", "")))
            choice.conditions = _dictionary_copy(choice_source.get("conditions", {}))
            choice.actions = _dictionary_copy(choice_source.get("actions", {}))
            choice.time_limit_seconds = float(choice_source.get("time_limit_seconds", -1.0))
            choices.append(choice)
        node.choices = choices
        nodes.append(node)
    dialogue.nodes = nodes
    if dialogue.entry_node_id == &"" and not nodes.is_empty():
        dialogue.entry_node_id = nodes[0].id

func _snake_case_dictionary(source: Dictionary) -> Dictionary:
    var result := {}
    for key in source:
        result[str(key).to_snake_case()] = source[key]
    return result

func _dictionary_copy(value: Variant) -> Dictionary:
    return (value as Dictionary).duplicate(true) if value is Dictionary else {}

func _resolve_resource(reference: Variant, expected_type: String) -> Resource:
    var reference_id := StringName("")
    if reference is String or reference is StringName:
        reference_id = StringName(reference)
    elif reference is Dictionary:
        reference_id = StringName(reference.get("id", ""))
    if reference_id == &"":
        return null
    var resource: Resource = resources_by_id.get(reference_id)
    if resource == null:
        _warning("Unresolved reference '%s' (expected %s)." % [reference_id, expected_type])
        return null
    var global_name := resource.get_script().get_global_name() if resource.get_script() != null else resource.get_class()
    if global_name != expected_type and not resource.is_class(expected_type):
        _warning("Reference '%s' is %s, expected %s." % [reference_id, global_name, expected_type])
    return resource

func _resolve_resource_array(references: Variant, expected_type: String) -> Array:
    var resolved: Array = []
    if not references is Array:
        return resolved
    for reference in references:
        var resource := _resolve_resource(reference, expected_type)
        if resource != null:
            resolved.append(resource)
    return resolved

func _save_resources() -> void:
    for definition_id in resources_by_id:
        var resource: Resource = resources_by_id[definition_id]
        var path: String = output_paths[definition_id]
        var result := ResourceSaver.save(resource, path)
        if result == OK:
            report.created += 1
        else:
            _error("Could not save '%s' to %s (error %s)." % [definition_id, path, result])

func _finish_report() -> Dictionary:
    var report_path := "res://migration/import-report.json"
    DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path("res://migration"))
    var file := FileAccess.open(report_path, FileAccess.WRITE)
    if file != null:
        file.store_string(JSON.stringify(report, "\t"))
    return report

func _safe_file_name(value: String) -> String:
    var result := value.to_lower()
    for character in [" ", "/", "\\", ":", "."]:
        result = result.replace(character, "_")
    return result

func _warning(message: String) -> void:
    report.warnings.append(message)
    push_warning("[Echo Importer] " + message)

func _error(message: String) -> void:
    report.errors.append(message)
    push_error("[Echo Importer] " + message)

================================================================================
// FILE: addons/echo_legacy_importer/plugin.cfg
================================================================================
[plugin]
name="Echo Legacy Importer"
description="Imports legacy Echo content and validates missions, chapters, dialogue and asset manifests."
author="OpenAI"
version="1.1.0"
script="plugin.gd"

================================================================================
// FILE: addons/echo_legacy_importer/plugin.gd
================================================================================
@tool
extends EditorPlugin

var file_dialog: FileDialog

func _enter_tree() -> void:
    add_tool_menu_item("Import Echo Legacy JSON…", _open_import_dialog)
    add_tool_menu_item("Validate Echo Content & Assets", _validate_project)

func _exit_tree() -> void:
    remove_tool_menu_item("Import Echo Legacy JSON…")
    remove_tool_menu_item("Validate Echo Content & Assets")
    if file_dialog != null:
        file_dialog.queue_free()

func _open_import_dialog() -> void:
    if file_dialog == null:
        file_dialog = FileDialog.new()
        file_dialog.file_mode = FileDialog.FILE_MODE_OPEN_FILE
        file_dialog.access = FileDialog.ACCESS_FILESYSTEM
        file_dialog.filters = PackedStringArray(["*.json ; JSON bundles"])
        file_dialog.file_selected.connect(_on_file_selected)
        get_editor_interface().get_base_control().add_child(file_dialog)
    file_dialog.popup_centered_ratio(0.7)

func _on_file_selected(path: String) -> void:
    var importer_script := load("res://addons/echo_legacy_importer/legacy_json_importer.gd")
    var importer = importer_script.new()
    var report: Dictionary = importer.import_file(path, "res://data/imported")
    get_editor_interface().get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    if report.get("errors", []).is_empty():
        print("[Echo Importer] Imported %s resources." % report.get("created", 0))
    else:
        push_error("[Echo Importer] Import completed with errors. See res://migration/import-report.json")

func _validate_project() -> void:
    var validator_script := load("res://scripts/tools/project_validator.gd")
    var validator = validator_script.new()
    var report: Dictionary = validator.validate_project()
    get_editor_interface().get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    if report.get("errors", []).is_empty():
        print("[Echo Validator] Passed with %d warning(s)." % report.get("warnings", []).size())
    else:
        push_error("[Echo Validator] Found %d error(s). See res://migration/validation-report.json" % report.get("errors", []).size())

// FILE: assets/ui/icons/README.md
================================================================================
# Echo UI Icons

Original SVG interface icons created for the Echo Godot project. They are included as project source assets and may be recoloured by Godot themes.

================================================================================
// FILE: data/assets/character_model_wrapper.tres
================================================================================
[gd_resource type="Resource" script_class="AssetManifestDefinition" load_steps=3 format=3]

// FILE: migration/ASSET_MIGRATOR_VALIDATION.md
================================================================================
# Asset migrator validation

Validated on July 4, 2026 with Python 3.13.

## Passed

- Python syntax compilation for the main migrator and Blender helper.
- Babylon `ImportMesh`, `Append`, `LoadAssetContainer` and `addMeshTask` root/file detection.
- Normal quoted asset paths and unquoted CSS/HTML URLs.
- Remote URL isolation without downloading.
- Dynamic template-path reporting.
- Local path aliases and web-root resolution.
- glTF `.bin` and external texture dependency preservation beside the parent model.
- Runtime folder classification for characters, textures, UI and audio.
- Reimport-safe wrapper generation with direct `source_scene` references.
- Provisional character socket generation.
- `AssetManifestDefinition` resource generation.
- PBR material generation from base-colour, normal and roughness maps.
- Existing Echo definition matching and conservative automatic binding preparation.
- JSON and Markdown report generation.

## Synthetic validation result

The test fixture contained one operator glTF with an external buffer and texture, a three-map hospital-floor PBR set, an audio file, a UI SVG, a remote model URL and a dynamic model template. The run completed with:

- 8 migrated files
- 1 model manifest and wrapper
- 2 generated materials
- 1 high-confidence `operator_alpha.model_scene` binding
- 0 missing local references
- 1 external reference correctly skipped
- 1 dynamic reference correctly flagged

## Remaining runtime checks

Godot and Blender executables were not available in this environment. After running against the actual Echo JavaScript source, open the project in Godot to complete imports and verify model scale, orientation, skeletons, animation names, texture colour space, generated materials, sockets, collisions and licences.

================================================================================
// FILE: migration/ASSET_PIPELINE.md
================================================================================
# Echo asset pipeline

## Directory policy

```text
assets/
├── source/          # Editable Blender, layered image and source-audio files
├── characters/
├── environments/
├── materials/
├── props/
├── audio/
├── ui/
└── vfx/
```

Large editable sources can live in Git LFS or an external art repository, but every runtime asset should have an `AssetManifestDefinition` in `data/`.

## 3D export standard

- Export runtime models as GLB/glTF.
- Use metres, +Y up and -Z forward.
- Apply object transforms before export.
- Use stable, descriptive mesh and material names.
- Keep collision meshes and LOD meshes identifiable by naming convention.
- Preserve armature and animation names across re-exports.

## Wrapper rule

Never attach gameplay scripts directly to an imported model scene. Godot may regenerate that scene when the source asset changes.

Instead:

1. Import the GLB.
2. Create or reuse a wrapper scene.
3. Put the imported scene beneath the wrapper's `VisualRoot`.
4. Keep gameplay scripts, collisions, sockets and metadata on the wrapper.
5. Reference the wrapper in an asset manifest.

`scenes/assets/character_model_wrapper.tscn` demonstrates the pattern.

## Character sockets

The standard character wrapper validates these sockets:

- `head`
- `left_hand`
- `right_hand`
- `weapon`
- `back`

Additional sockets can be declared in `required_sockets` on the manifest.

## Asset manifest fields

- `id`, `display_name`, `description`
- `source_path`: editable source location
- `imported_path`: Godot runtime resource path
- `wrapper_scene`: stable gameplay wrapper
- `asset_type`
- `license_name`, `license_source`, `author_name`
- `scale_multiplier`
- `required_sockets`
- `material_overrides`
- `metadata`

Material overrides map a `MeshInstance3D` node name to a material resource path.

## Validation workflow

1. Import or reimport the asset.
2. Confirm scale, orientation, animation and materials.
3. Open the wrapper and align sockets/collision.
4. Update the asset manifest and license metadata.
5. Run **Tools → Validate Echo Content & Assets**.
6. Resolve every error before committing. Review warnings for missing licensing or objective targets.

================================================================================

          }

  }

  function isSolelyIdentifierDefinitionLocation(contextToken2) {

  function filterObjectMembersList(contextualMemberSymbols, existingMembers) {
    if (existingMembers.length === 0) {
      return contextualMemberSymbols;
    }
    const membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();

      if (m.kind !== 304 /* PropertyAssignment */ && m.kind !== 305 /* ShorthandPropertyAssignment */ && m.kind !== 209 /* BindingElement */ && m.kind !== 175 /* MethodDeclaration */ && m.kind !== 178 /* GetAccessor */ && m.kind !== 179 /* SetAccessor */ && m.kind !== 306 /* SpreadAssignment */) {
        continue;
      }

        continue;
      }

        }

        existingName = name && isPropertyNameLiteral(name) ? getEscapedTextOfIdentifierOrLiteral(name) : void 0;
      }

        existingMemberNames.add(existingName);
      }
    }

    setSortTextToMemberDeclaredBySpreadAssignment(membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment, filteredSymbols);
    return filteredSymbols;
  }
  function setMembersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment(declaration, membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment) {
    const expression = declaration.expression;
    const symbol = typeChecker.getSymbolAtLocation(expression);

    const properties = type && type.properties;
    if (properties) {
      properties.forEach((property) => {
        membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment.add(property.name);
      });
    }
  }
  function setSortTextToOptionalMember() {
    symbols.forEach((m) => {
      if (m.flags & 16777216 /* Optional */) {
        const symbolId = getSymbolId(m);
        symbolToSortTextMap[symbolId] = symbolToSortTextMap[symbolId] ?? SortText.OptionalMember;
      }
    });
  }
  function setSortTextToMemberDeclaredBySpreadAssignment(membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment, contextualMemberSymbols) {
    if (membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment.size === 0) {
      return;
    }
    for (const contextualMemberSymbol of contextualMemberSymbols) {
      if (membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment.has(contextualMemberSymbol.name)) {
        symbolToSortTextMap[getSymbolId(contextualMemberSymbol)] = SortText.MemberDeclaredBySpreadAssignment;
      }
    }
  }
  function transformObjectLiteralMembersSortText(start2) {

      }
    }
  }
  function filterClassMembersList(baseSymbols, existingMembers, currentClassElementModifierFlags) {
    const existingMemberNames = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();
    for (const m of existingMembers) {
      if (m.kind !== 173 /* PropertyDeclaration */ && m.kind !== 175 /* MethodDeclaration */ && m.kind !== 178 /* GetAccessor */ && m.kind !== 179 /* SetAccessor */) {
        continue;
      }
      if (isCurrentlyEditingNode(m)) {
        continue;
      }
      if (hasEffectiveModifier(m, 2 /* Private */)) {
        continue;
      }
      if (isStatic(m) !== !!(currentClassElementModifierFlags & 256 /* Static */)) {
        continue;
      }
      const existingName = getPropertyNameForPropertyNameNode(m.name);
      if (existingName) {
        existingMemberNames.add(existingName);
      }
    }
    return baseSymbols.filter(
      (propertySymbol) => !existingMemberNames.has(propertySymbol.escapedName) && !!propertySymbol.declarations && !(getDeclarationModifierFlagsFromSymbol(propertySymbol) & 2 /* Private */) && !(propertySymbol.valueDeclaration && isPrivateIdentifierClassElementDeclaration(propertySymbol.valueDeclaration))
    );
  }
  function filterJsxAttributes(symbols2, attributes) {
    const seenNames = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();
    const membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment = /* @__PURE__ */ new Set();
    for (const attr of attributes) {
      if (isCurrentlyEditingNode(attr)) {
        continue;
      }
      if (attr.kind === 292 /* JsxAttribute */) {
        seenNames.add(getEscapedTextOfJsxAttributeName(attr.name));
      } else if (isJsxSpreadAttribute(attr)) {
        setMembersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment(attr, membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment);
      }
    }
    const filteredSymbols = symbols2.filter((a) => !seenNames.has(a.escapedName));
    setSortTextToMemberDeclaredBySpreadAssignment(membersDeclaredBySpreadAssignment, filteredSymbols);
    return filteredSymbols;
  }
  function isCurrentlyEditingNode(node2) {
    return node2.getStart(sourceFile) <= position && position <= node2.getEnd();
  }

function tryGetObjectLikeCompletionContainer(contextToken, position, sourceFile) {
  var _a;
  if (contextToken) {
    const { parent: parent2 } = contextToken;
    switch (contextToken.kind) {
      case 19 /* OpenBraceToken */:
      // const x = { |
      case 28 /* CommaToken */:
        if (isObjectLiteralExpression(parent2) || isObjectBindingPattern(parent2)) {
          return parent2;
        }
        break;
      case 42 /* AsteriskToken */:
        return isMethodDeclaration(parent2) ? tryCast(parent2.parent, isObjectLiteralExpression) : void 0;
      case 134 /* AsyncKeyword */:
        return tryCast(parent2.parent, isObjectLiteralExpression);
      case 80 /* Identifier */:
        if (contextToken.text === "async" && isShorthandPropertyAssignment(contextToken.parent)) {
          return contextToken.parent.parent;
        } else {
          if (isObjectLiteralExpression(contextToken.parent.parent) && (isSpreadAssignment(contextToken.parent) || isShorthandPropertyAssignment(contextToken.parent) && getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile, contextToken.getEnd()).line !== getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile, position).line)) {
            return contextToken.parent.parent;
          }
          const ancestorNode2 = findAncestor(parent2, isPropertyAssignment);
          if ((ancestorNode2 == null ? void 0 : ancestorNode2.getLastToken(sourceFile)) === contextToken && isObjectLiteralExpression(ancestorNode2.parent)) {
            return ancestorNode2.parent;
          }
        }
        break;
      default:
        if (((_a = parent2.parent) == null ? void 0 : _a.parent) && (isMethodDeclaration(parent2.parent) || isGetAccessorDeclaration(parent2.parent) || isSetAccessorDeclaration(parent2.parent)) && isObjectLiteralExpression(parent2.parent.parent)) {
          return parent2.parent.parent;
        }
        if (isSpreadAssignment(parent2) && isObjectLiteralExpression(parent2.parent)) {
          return parent2.parent;
        }
        const ancestorNode = findAncestor(parent2, isPropertyAssignment);
        if (contextToken.kind !== 59 /* ColonToken */ && (ancestorNode == null ? void 0 : ancestorNode.getLastToken(sourceFile)) === contextToken && isObjectLiteralExpression(ancestorNode.parent)) {
          return ancestorNode.parent;
        }
    }
  }
  return void 0;
}
function getRelevantTokens(position, sourceFile) {
  const previousToken = findPrecedingToken(position, sourceFile);
  if (previousToken && position <= previousToken.end && (isMemberName(previousToken) || isKeyword(previousToken.kind))) {
    const contextToken = findPrecedingToken(
      previousToken.getFullStart(),
      sourceFile,
      /*startNode*/
      void 0
    );
    return { contextToken, previousToken };
  }
  return { contextToken: previousToken, previousToken };
}
function getAutoImportSymbolFromCompletionEntryData(name, data, program, host) {
  const containingProgram = data.isPackageJsonImport ? host.getPackageJsonAutoImportProvider() : program;
  const checker = containingProgram.getTypeChecker();
  const moduleSymbol = data.ambientModuleName ? checker.tryFindAmbientModule(data.ambientModuleName) : data.fileName ? checker.getMergedSymbol(Debug.checkDefined(containingProgram.getSourceFile(data.fileName)).symbol) : void 0;
  if (!moduleSymbol) return void 0;
  let symbol = data.exportName === "export=" /* ExportEquals */ ? checker.resolveExternalModuleSymbol(moduleSymbol) : checker.tryGetMemberInModuleExportsAndProperties(data.exportName, moduleSymbol);
  if (!symbol) return void 0;
  const isDefaultExport = data.exportName === "default" /* Default */;
  symbol = isDefaultExport && getLocalSymbolForExportDefault(symbol) || symbol;
  return { symbol, origin: completionEntryDataToSymbolOriginInfo(data, name, moduleSymbol) };
}
function getCompletionEntryDisplayNameForSymbol(symbol, target, origin, kind, jsxIdentifierExpected) {
  if (originIsIgnore(origin)) {
    return void 0;
  }
  const name = originIncludesSymbolName(origin) ? origin.symbolName : symbol.name;
  if (name === void 0 || symbol.flags & 1536 /* Module */ && isSingleOrDoubleQuote(name.charCodeAt(0)) || isKnownSymbol(symbol)) {
    return void 0;
  }
  const validNameResult = { name, needsConvertPropertyAccess: false };
  if (isIdentifierText(name, target, jsxIdentifierExpected ? 1 /* JSX */ : 0 /* Standard */) || symbol.valueDeclaration && isPrivateIdentifierClassElementDeclaration(symbol.valueDeclaration)) {
    return validNameResult;
  }
  if (symbol.flags & 2097152 /* Alias */) {
    return { name, needsConvertPropertyAccess: true };
  }
  switch (kind) {
    case 3 /* MemberLike */:
      return originIsComputedPropertyName(origin) ? { name: origin.symbolName, needsConvertPropertyAccess: false } : void 0;
    case 0 /* ObjectPropertyDeclaration */:
      return { name: JSON.stringify(name), needsConvertPropertyAccess: false };
    case 2 /* PropertyAccess */:
    case 1 /* Global */:
      return name.charCodeAt(0) === 32 /* space */ ? void 0 : { name, needsConvertPropertyAccess: true };
    case 5 /* None */:
    case 4 /* String */:
      return validNameResult;
    default:
      Debug.assertNever(kind);
  }
}
var _keywordCompletions = [];
var allKeywordsCompletions = memoize(() => {
  const res = [];
  for (let i = 83 /* FirstKeyword */; i <= 166 /* LastKeyword */; i++) {
    res.push({
      name: tokenToString(i),
      kind: "keyword" /* keyword */,
      kindModifiers: "" /* none */,
      sortText: SortText.GlobalsOrKeywords
    });
  }
  return res;
});
function getKeywordCompletions(keywordFilter, filterOutTsOnlyKeywords) {
  if (!filterOutTsOnlyKeywords) return getTypescriptKeywordCompletions(keywordFilter);
  const index = keywordFilter + 8 /* Last */ + 1;
  return _keywordCompletions[index] || (_keywordCompletions[index] = getTypescriptKeywordCompletions(keywordFilter).filter((entry) => !isTypeScriptOnlyKeyword(stringToToken(entry.name))));
}
function getTypescriptKeywordCompletions(keywordFilter) {
  return _keywordCompletions[keywordFilter] || (_keywordCompletions[keywordFilter] = allKeywordsCompletions().filter((entry) => {
    const kind = stringToToken(entry.name);
    switch (keywordFilter) {
      case 0 /* None */:
        return false;
      case 1 /* All */:
        return isFunctionLikeBodyKeyword(kind) || kind === 138 /* DeclareKeyword */ || kind === 144 /* ModuleKeyword */ || kind === 156 /* TypeKeyword */ || kind === 145 /* NamespaceKeyword */ || kind === 128 /* AbstractKeyword */ || isTypeKeyword(kind) && kind !== 157 /* UndefinedKeyword */;
      case 5 /* FunctionLikeBodyKeywords */:
        return isFunctionLikeBodyKeyword(kind);
      case 2 /* ClassElementKeywords */:
        return isClassMemberCompletionKeyword(kind);
      case 3 /* InterfaceElementKeywords */:
        return isInterfaceOrTypeLiteralCompletionKeyword(kind);
      case 4 /* ConstructorParameterKeywords */:
        return isParameterPropertyModifier(kind);
      case 6 /* TypeAssertionKeywords */:
        return isTypeKeyword(kind) || kind === 87 /* ConstKeyword */;
      case 7 /* TypeKeywords */:
        return isTypeKeyword(kind);
      case 8 /* TypeKeyword */:
        return kind === 156 /* TypeKeyword */;
      default:
        return Debug.assertNever(keywordFilter);
    }
  }));
}
function isTypeScriptOnlyKeyword(kind) {
  switch (kind) {
    case 128 /* AbstractKeyword */:
    case 133 /* AnyKeyword */:
    case 163 /* BigIntKeyword */:
    case 136 /* BooleanKeyword */:
    case 138 /* DeclareKeyword */:
    case 94 /* EnumKeyword */:
    case 162 /* GlobalKeyword */:
    case 119 /* ImplementsKeyword */:
    case 140 /* InferKeyword */:
    case 120 /* InterfaceKeyword */:
    case 142 /* IsKeyword */:
    case 143 /* KeyOfKeyword */:
    case 144 /* ModuleKeyword */:
    case 145 /* NamespaceKeyword */:
    case 146 /* NeverKeyword */:
    case 150 /* NumberKeyword */:
    case 151 /* ObjectKeyword */:
    case 164 /* OverrideKeyword */:
    case 123 /* PrivateKeyword */:
    case 124 /* ProtectedKeyword */:
    case 125 /* PublicKeyword */:
    case 148 /* ReadonlyKeyword */:
    case 154 /* StringKeyword */:
    case 155 /* SymbolKeyword */:
    case 156 /* TypeKeyword */:
    case 158 /* UniqueKeyword */:
    case 159 /* UnknownKeyword */:
      return true;
    default:
      return false;
  }
}
function isInterfaceOrTypeLiteralCompletionKeyword(kind) {
  return kind === 148 /* ReadonlyKeyword */;
}
function isClassMemberCompletionKeyword(kind) {
  switch (kind) {
    case 128 /* AbstractKeyword */:
    case 129 /* AccessorKeyword */:
    case 137 /* ConstructorKeyword */:
    case 139 /* GetKeyword */:
    case 153 /* SetKeyword */:
    case 134 /* AsyncKeyword */:
    case 138 /* DeclareKeyword */:
    case 164 /* OverrideKeyword */:
      return true;
    default:
      return isClassMemberModifier(kind);
  }
}
function isFunctionLikeBodyKeyword(kind) {
  return kind === 134 /* AsyncKeyword */ || kind === 135 /* AwaitKeyword */ || kind === 160 /* UsingKeyword */ || kind === 130 /* AsKeyword */ || kind === 152 /* SatisfiesKeyword */ || kind === 156 /* TypeKeyword */ || !isContextualKeyword(kind) && !isClassMemberCompletionKeyword(kind);
}
function keywordForNode(node) {
  return isIdentifier(node) ? identifierToKeywordKind(node) ?? 0 /* Unknown */ : node.kind;
}
function getContextualKeywords(contextToken, position) {
  const entries = [];

    const file = contextToken.getSourceFile();
    const parent2 = contextToken.parent;
    const tokenLine = file.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(contextToken.end).line;
    const currentLine = file.getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(position).line;
    if ((isImportDeclaration(parent2) || isExportDeclaration(parent2) && parent2.moduleSpecifier) && contextToken === parent2.moduleSpecifier && tokenLine === currentLine) {
      entries.push({
        name: tokenToString(132 /* AssertKeyword */),
        kind: "keyword" /* keyword */,
        kindModifiers: "" /* none */,
        sortText: SortText.GlobalsOrKeywords
      });
    }
  }
  return entries;
}
function getJsDocTagAtPosition(node, position) {
  return findAncestor(node, (n) => isJSDocTag(n) && rangeContainsPosition(n, position) ? true : isJSDoc(n) ? "quit" : false);
}
function getPropertiesForObjectExpression(contextualType, completionsType, obj, checker) {
  const hasCompletionsType = completionsType && completionsType !== contextualType;
  const promiseFilteredContextualType = checker.getUnionType(
    filter(
      contextualType.flags & 134217728 /* Union */ ? contextualType.types : [contextualType],
      (t) => !checker.getPromisedTypeOfPromise(t)
    )
  );
  const type = hasCompletionsType && !(completionsType.flags & 3 /* AnyOrUnknown */) ? checker.getUnionType([promiseFilteredContextualType, completionsType]) : promiseFilteredContextualType;
  const properties = getApparentProperties(type, obj, checker);
  return type.isClass() && containsNonPublicProperties(properties) ? [] : hasCompletionsType ? filter(properties, hasDeclarationOtherThanSelf) : properties;
  function hasDeclarationOtherThanSelf(member) {
    if (!length(member.declarations)) return true;
    return some(member.declarations, (decl) => decl.parent !== obj);
  }
}
function getApparentProperties(type, node, checker) {
  if (!type.isUnion()) return type.getApparentProperties();
  return checker.getAllPossiblePropertiesOfTypes(filter(type.types, (memberType) => !(memberType.flags & 12713980 /* Primitive */ || checker.isArrayLikeType(memberType) || checker.isTypeInvalidDueToUnionDiscriminant(memberType, node) || checker.typeHasCallOrConstructSignatures(memberType) || memberType.isClass() && containsNonPublicProperties(memberType.getApparentProperties()))));
}
function containsNonPublicProperties(props) {
  return some(props, (p) => !!(getDeclarationModifierFlagsFromSymbol(p) & 6 /* NonPublicAccessibilityModifier */));
}
function getPropertiesForCompletion(type, checker) {
  return type.isUnion() ? Debug.checkEachDefined(checker.getAllPossiblePropertiesOfTypes(type.types), "getAllPossiblePropertiesOfTypes() should all be defined") : Debug.checkEachDefined(type.getApparentProperties(), "getApparentProperties() should all be defined");
}
function tryGetObjectTypeDeclarationCompletionContainer(sourceFile, contextToken, location, position) {
  switch (location.kind) {
    case 353 /* SyntaxList */:
      return tryCast(location.parent, isObjectTypeDeclaration);
    case 1 /* EndOfFileToken */:
      const cls = tryCast(lastOrUndefined(cast(location.parent, isSourceFile).statements), isObjectTypeDeclaration);
      if (cls && !findChildOfKind(cls, 20 /* CloseBraceToken */, sourceFile)) {
        return cls;
      }
      break;
    case 81 /* PrivateIdentifier */:
      if (tryCast(location.parent, isPropertyDeclaration)) {
        return findAncestor(location, isClassLike);
      }
      break;
    case 80 /* Identifier */: {
      const originalKeywordKind = identifierToKeywordKind(location);
      if (originalKeywordKind) {
        return void 0;
      }
      if (isPropertyDeclaration(location.parent) && location.parent.initializer === location) {
        return void 0;
      }
      if (isFromObjectTypeDeclaration(location)) {
        return findAncestor(location, isObjectTypeDeclaration);
      }
    }
  }
  if (!contextToken) return void 0;
  if (location.kind === 137 /* ConstructorKeyword */ || isIdentifier(contextToken) && isPropertyDeclaration(contextToken.parent) && isClassLike(location)) {
    return findAncestor(contextToken, isClassLike);
  }
  switch (contextToken.kind) {
    case 64 /* EqualsToken */:
      return void 0;
    case 27 /* SemicolonToken */:
    // class c {getValue(): number; | }
    case 20 /* CloseBraceToken */:
      return isFromObjectTypeDeclaration(location) && location.parent.name === location ? location.parent.parent : tryCast(location, isObjectTypeDeclaration);
    case 19 /* OpenBraceToken */:
    // class c { |
    case 28 /* CommaToken */:
      return tryCast(contextToken.parent, isObjectTypeDeclaration);
    default:
      if (isObjectTypeDeclaration(location)) {
        if (getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile, contextToken.getEnd()).line !== getLineAndCharacterOfPosition(sourceFile, position).line) {
          return location;
        }
        const isValidKeyword = isClassLike(contextToken.parent.parent) ? isClassMemberCompletionKeyword : isInterfaceOrTypeLiteralCompletionKeyword;
        return isValidKeyword(contextToken.kind) || contextToken.kind === 42 /* AsteriskToken */ || isIdentifier(contextToken) && isValidKeyword(identifierToKeywordKind(contextToken) ?? 0 /* Unknown */) ? contextToken.parent.parent : void 0;
      }
      return void 0;
  }
}
function tryGetTypeLiteralNode(node) {
  if (!node) return void 0;
  const parent2 = node.parent;
  switch (node.kind) {
    case 19 /* OpenBraceToken */:
      if (isTypeLiteralNode(parent2)) {
        return parent2;
      }
      break;
    case 27 /* SemicolonToken */:
    case 28 /* CommaToken */:
    case 80 /* Identifier */:
      if (parent2.kind === 172 /* PropertySignature */ && isTypeLiteralNode(parent2.parent)) {
        return parent2.parent;
      }
      break;
  }
  return void 0;
}
function getConstraintOfTypeArgumentProperty(node, checker) {
  if (!node) return void 0;
  if (isTypeNode(node)) {
    const constraint = checker.getTypeArgumentConstraint(node);
    if (constraint) return constraint;
  }
  const t = getConstraintOfTypeArgumentProperty(node.parent, checker);
  if (!t) return void 0;
  switch (node.kind) {
    case 172 /* PropertySignature */:
      return checker.getTypeOfPropertyOfContextualType(t, node.symbol.escapedName);
    case 59 /* ColonToken */:
      if (node.parent.kind === 172 /* PropertySignature */) {
        return t;
      }
      break;
    case 194 /* IntersectionType */:
    case 188 /* TypeLiteral */:
    case 193 /* UnionType */:
      return t;
    case 23 /* OpenBracketToken */:
      return checker.getElementTypeOfArrayType(t);
  }
}
function isFromObjectTypeDeclaration(node) {
  return node.parent && isClassOrTypeElement(node.parent) && isObjectTypeDeclaration(node.parent.parent);
}

  switch (triggerCharacter) {
    case ".":
    case "@":
      return true;
    case '"':
    case "'":
    case "` at `node_modules/typescript/lib/typescript.js:170382`

## Required manual checks

1. Open Godot and allow it to import all copied files.
2. Check model scale, +Y up orientation, -Z forward direction, materials, skeletons and animations.
3. Align generated character sockets to the imported skeleton or mesh.
4. Replace every `Unknown - review required` licence entry with verified metadata.
5. Review `.babylon` files and failed Blender conversions; convert these to GLB manually.
6. Apply configured unit/weapon/equipment bindings with `apply_asset_bindings.gd`.
7. Run Echo's **Validate Content & Assets** developer tool.

// FILE: migration/implementation-notes.md
================================================================================
# Implementation notes — phases 1–11

## Migration strategy

The Godot project remains additive. The Babylon prototype can stay operational while definitions, assets and maps are migrated incrementally.

## Tactical ownership

Input and AI create command objects. Commands invoke unit components. UI displays and issues commands but does not contain combat rules. Each unit owns its queue; `CommandCoordinator` handles synchronization barriers and Jammer disruption.

## Prague mission state

`MissionRuntime` owns objective state, crisis timing, observer escape, extraction gating and result generation. Objective definitions remain data resources, while map targets use reusable `MissionInteractable` and `ObjectiveTrigger` nodes.

The crisis is intentionally exclusive. Completing one priority resolves the crisis and fails the competing objectives. Extraction requires every living operator inside the extraction zone.

## Strategic persistence

`StrategicSystem` normalizes save data and owns research, construction, facilities, power, intelligence, inventory, loadouts and personnel recovery. Mission results carry campaign actions and unit results back to the strategic layer before an automatic save.

## Narrative architecture

`StoryState` evaluates reusable condition dictionaries and applies action dictionaries. `DialogueManager` handles conditional nodes, choices and timers. `ChapterManager` resolves mission availability, one-shot events, chapter opening dialogue, progression and failure.

## Asset architecture

Imported scenes are visual inputs, not gameplay owners. `AssetManifestDefinition` records provenance and import metadata. `AssetWrapper` protects sockets, collision and scripts from reimport. The editor validator checks content and asset references together.

## Production navigation

The greybox can fall back to direct movement when no synchronized navigation map exists. Final maps should use baked `NavigationRegion3D`, door traversal links and cover-aware route evaluation.

================================================================================

  "warnings": [],
  "errors": [],
  "note": "This file is replaced whenever the legacy JSON importer runs."

// FILE: migration/JS_TO_GODOT_ASSET_MIGRATION.md
================================================================================
# Babylon.js → Godot asset migration

This toolkit migrates asset files from the legacy Echo Babylon.js project into the phase 1–14 Godot project. It does not translate Babylon scene logic; it moves runtime art and produces the Godot metadata needed to integrate it safely.

## What the scanner recognizes

- Babylon `SceneLoader.ImportMesh`, `ImportMeshAsync`, `Append`, `LoadAssetContainer` and `AssetsManager.addMeshTask` root/file pairs
- Normal JavaScript, TypeScript, HTML, CSS and JSON string paths
- Models: GLB, glTF, FBX, OBJ, DAE, BLEND and `.babylon`
- Textures: PNG, JPEG, WebP, SVG, TGA, HDR, EXR, DDS and KTX/KTX2
- Audio, fonts and video
- glTF external buffers and image dependencies
- Dynamic template paths, remote URLs and missing files, which are listed for manual resolution

## Generated Godot output

```text
assets/
├── characters/
├── environments/
├── props/
├── textures/
├── audio/{music,sfx,voice}/
├── ui/
├── vfx/
└── source/legacy_models/

scenes/assets/generated/       # reimport-safe wrappers
data/assets/generated/         # AssetManifestDefinition resources
assets/materials/generated/    # detected PBR material sets
migration/generated/           # reports, indexes and bindings
```

Character wrappers include provisional `head`, `left_hand`, `right_hand`, `weapon` and `back` sockets. Their positions must be aligned after the real model imports.

## Recommended workflow

### 1. Make a configuration

Copy:

```text
migration/tools/asset-migration.config.example.json
```

Edit path aliases, classification keywords, licence defaults, scale overrides and definition bindings. A binding connects an imported wrapper or texture to a field such as:

- `operator_alpha.model_scene`
- `operator_alpha.portrait`
- `service_rifle.model_scene`
- `smoke_grenade.icon`

### 2. Run a dry scan

```bash
python migration/tools/migrate_babylon_assets.py \
  --source-root /path/to/echo-js \
  --godot-root /path/to/echo-godot-phase1-14 \
  --config migration/tools/asset-migration.config.json
```

Review `migration/generated/asset-migration-report.md` before copying anything.

### 3. Apply the migration

Linux/macOS:

```bash
./migration/tools/run_asset_migration.sh \
  /path/to/echo-js \
  /path/to/echo-godot-phase1-14 \
  /path/to/asset-migration.config.json
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
./migration/tools/run_asset_migration.ps1 `
  -JsRoot "C:\projects\echo-js" `
  -GodotRoot "C:\projects\echo-godot-phase1-14" `
  -Config "C:\projects\asset-migration.config.json" `
  -Blender "C:\Program Files\Blender Foundation\Blender 4.5\blender.exe"
```

The Python command can also be run directly with `--apply`. Add `--auto-bind` to prepare only high-confidence name matches such as `operator_alpha_model` → `operator_alpha.model_scene`; review `suggested-asset-bindings.json` before applying them.

### 4. Let Godot import files

Open the Godot project and wait for the filesystem import to finish. Model resources do not exist to Godot until this import completes.

### 5. Apply definition bindings

After imports finish:

1. Open `migration/tools/apply_asset_bindings.gd` in Godot.
2. Use **File → Run** in the script editor, or run it as an `EditorScript`.
3. Review `migration/generated/asset-binding-report.json`.

### 6. Validate and review

Use the F10 developer workbench and run **Validate Content & Assets**. Check:

- metres and model scale
- +Y up and -Z forward
- skeleton and animation names
- material texture channels
- collision and navigation
- character socket alignment
- asset licences

## Model conversion

GLB/glTF files are copied directly. FBX, OBJ, DAE and BLEND are converted to GLB when Blender is installed and `--convert-models auto` is used. Provide Blender explicitly with `--blender` when it is not on `PATH`.

`.babylon` scene files cannot be reliably translated by this script. They are copied into `assets/source/legacy_models/` and reported for manual export to GLB from the originating DCC application or a Babylon-compatible converter.

## Safety

- The first run should be a dry run.
- Existing generated files are preserved unless `--overwrite` is supplied.
- Identical files are deduplicated by SHA-256.
- Remote URLs are never downloaded automatically.
- Every generated manifest defaults to licence review unless you set verified values in the configuration.

// FILE: migration/TESTING_AND_PROFILING.md
================================================================================
# Testing and profiling workflow

## Automated suite

Run `tools/run_tests.sh` or `tools/run_tests.ps1`. The test runner exits with code 0 only when every assertion passes.

Coverage includes:

- Health, healing, invulnerability and lethal damage
- Tactical hit estimates and confidence bands
- Suppression thresholds and movement/accuracy penalties
- Tactical command lifecycle and validation
- Mission objective prerequisites, crisis exclusivity and extraction activation
- Story flags, conditions and actions
- Dialogue graph integrity and transitions
- Research prerequisites, costs and equipment inventory transfers
- AI target-scoring priorities
- Save serialization round trips
- Project content and reference validation

## Scenario laboratories

Open the developer workbench with F10 and choose a laboratory:

| Laboratory | Purpose |
|---|---|
| Movement | Selection, formation movement, stances and final facing |
| Cover | Cover scoring, protection and line of fire |
| Overwatch | Cone visualization and reaction-fire timing |
| Door | Open, lock, hack, breach and destruction states |
| Smoke + EMP | Visibility blocking and electronic disruption |
| Hunter AI | Isolation targeting and flanking |
| Jammer AI | Command disruption and EMP counterplay |
| Objective | Mission transitions and extraction gating |
| Performance | Additional enemy load for profiling |

## Performance overlay

Press F11 to display:

- Current and rolling average FPS
- Current and rolling average frame time
- Script/process time
- Physics time
- Navigation time
- Static memory
- Live object count
- Draw calls
- Rendered primitives
- Active quality profile

## Quality profiles

- **Low:** slower AI/perception updates, reduced particles and two shadow-casting lights.
- **Balanced:** default tactical update cadence and four shadow-casting lights.
- **High:** faster tactical sensing with full particles and up to eight shadow-casting lights.
- **Auto:** changes profile from rolling frame-rate samples.

## Profiling checklist

1. Launch the Performance Lab from F10.
2. Enable the F11 overlay.
3. Test normal speed and tactical pause.
4. Trigger smoke, EMP, suppression and overwatch together.
5. Compare Low, Balanced, High and Auto profiles.
6. Watch navigation and process time while the stress units are active.
7. Capture a screenshot with F12 and record the profile and frame counters.
8. Repeat after replacing greybox assets with final models and materials.

Do not optimize based solely on editor performance. Verify release exports on each target platform with final assets and representative mission population.

================================================================================
// FILE: migration/tools/apply_asset_bindings.gd
================================================================================
@tool
extends EditorScript

const BINDINGS_PATH := "res://migration/generated/asset-bindings.json"
const DATA_ROOT := "res://data"

func _run() -> void:
    var report := {
        "applied": [],
        "warnings": [],
        "errors": [],
    }
    if not FileAccess.file_exists(BINDINGS_PATH):
        push_error("Asset bindings file does not exist: %s" % BINDINGS_PATH)
        return
    var file := FileAccess.open(BINDINGS_PATH, FileAccess.READ)
    if file == null:
        push_error("Could not open asset bindings file.")
        return
    var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(file.get_as_text())
    if not parsed is Dictionary:
        push_error("Asset bindings JSON root must be an object.")
        return
    var definitions := _index_definitions(DATA_ROOT)
    for raw_binding in parsed.get("bindings", []):
        if not raw_binding is Dictionary:
            report.warnings.append("Skipped non-object binding.")
            continue
        var definition_id := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("definition_id", "")))
        var field := StringName(str(raw_binding.get("field", "")))
        var resource_path := str(raw_binding.get("resource_path", ""))
        var entry: Dictionary = definitions.get(definition_id, {})
        var resource: Resource = entry.get("resource")
        var source_path := str(entry.get("path", ""))
        if resource == null:
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' was not found." % definition_id)
            continue
        if not _has_property(resource, field):
            report.errors.append("Definition '%s' has no field '%s'." % [definition_id, field])
            continue
        if resource_path.is_empty() or not ResourceLoader.exists(resource_path):
            report.errors.append("Binding resource does not exist: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        var value := ResourceLoader.load(resource_path)
        if value == null:
            report.errors.append("Could not load binding resource: %s" % resource_path)
            continue
        resource.set(field, value)
        var save_result := ResourceSaver.save(resource, source_path)
        if save_result != OK:
            report.errors.append("Could not save '%s' after binding '%s'." % [definition_id, field])
            continue
        report.applied.append({
            "definition_id": definition_id,
            "field": field,
            "resource_path": resource_path,
        })
    _write_report(report)
    EditorInterface.get_resource_filesystem().scan()
    print("Echo asset bindings: %d applied, %d warning(s), %d error(s)." % [
        report.applied.size(), report.warnings.size(), report.errors.size()
    ])
    for message in report.warnings:
        push_warning(message)
    for message in report.errors:
        push_error(message)

func _index_definitions(path: String) -> Dictionary:
    var result := {}
    var directory := DirAccess.open(path)
    if directory == null:
        return result
    directory.list_dir_begin()
    var entry := directory.get_next()
    while not entry.is_empty():
        if entry.begins_with("."):
            entry = directory.get_next()
            continue
        var full_path := path.path_join(entry)
        if directory.current_is_dir():
            result.merge(_index_definitions(full_path), false)
        elif entry.get_extension().to_lower() in ["tres", "res"]:
            var resource := ResourceLoader.load(full_path)
            if resource != null and _has_property(resource, &"id"):
                var definition_id: StringName = resource.get("id")
                if definition_id != &"":
                    result[definition_id] = {"resource": resource, "path": full_path}
        entry = directory.get_next()
    directory.list_dir_end()
    return result

func _has_property(object: Object, property_name: StringName) -> bool:
    for property in object.get_property_list():
        if StringName(property.name) == property_name:
            return true
    return false

func _write_report(report: Dictionary) -> void:
    DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path("res://migration/generated"))
    var output := FileAccess.open("res://migration/generated/asset-binding-report.json", FileAccess.WRITE)
    if output != null:
        output.store_string(JSON.stringify(report, "\t"))

================================================================================
// FILE: migration/tools/asset-migration.config.example.json
================================================================================
{
  "include_roots": [
    "assets",
    "public/assets",
    "src/assets",
    "public/models",
    "public/textures",
    "public/audio"
  ],
  "exclude_globs": [
    "**/node_modules/**",
    "**/.git/**",
    "**/dist/**",
    "**/build/**"
  ],
  "path_aliases": {
    "@/": ["src/"],
    "~/": ["src/"],
    "/assets/": ["public/assets/", "assets/", "src/assets/"],
    "/models/": ["public/models/", "assets/models/", "models/"],
    "/textures/": ["public/textures/", "assets/textures/", "textures/"],
    "/audio/": ["public/audio/", "assets/audio/", "audio/"]
  },
  "classification_rules": {
    "characters": ["operator", "guard", "hunter", "jammer", "observer", "patient", "doctor", "nurse"],
    "environments": ["hospital", "corridor", "reception", "icu", "server", "utility", "door", "wall", "floor"],
    "ui": ["ui", "hud", "icon", "portrait", "logo"],
    "vfx": ["vfx", "particle", "smoke", "spark", "muzzle", "decal"],
    "music": ["music", "score", "theme", "ambient"],
    "voice": ["voice", "dialogue", "narration"],

// FILE: PHASES_12_14_CHANGELOG.md
================================================================================
# Echo Godot — phases 12–14 changelog

## Phase 12 — Tactical interface

- Replaced the fixed prototype HUD with a container-based tactical interface.
- Added an objective/crisis header, selected-unit details, squad roster, command queue and contextual action dock.
- Added an interactive minimap and event-driven notification feed.
- Added action icons for movement, fire modes, overwatch, smoke, EMP, medkit, reload, interaction, pause and extraction.
- Added target previews with hit chance, expected damage, suppression, range, line of sight and movement timing.
- Added keyboard squad selection with F1–F3.
- Added settings for UI scale, high contrast, tactical probability numbers and reduced motion.
- Added low, balanced, high and automatic performance profiles to the main settings menu.

## Phase 13 — Developer workbench

- Added a global F10 developer workbench that pauses tactical play while open.
- Added mission and scenario-lab launching.
- Added mission-objective editing and forced extraction readiness.
- Added dialogue graph inspection and dialogue launching.
- Added story-flag and full campaign-state JSON editors.
- Added enemy spawning, equipment grants, healing, killing, suppression clearing, queue clearing and AI alert controls.
- Added god mode, fog reveal, roof reveal and AI enable toggles.
- Added cover, AI-path, perception, sound-event, objective-target and navigation-intent visualization.
- Added named save snapshots, restore/delete controls and main-save support.
- Added screenshot capture under `user://screenshots`.
- Added project validation, runtime sanity checks, console inspection and a live performance overlay.

## Phase 14 — Testing and optimization

- Added 15 GDScript test files and a headless scene-based test runner.
- Added test coverage for health/damage, tactical estimates, suppression, story conditions/actions, dialogue links, save round trips, command validation, objective transitions, content validation, AI scoring and strategic progression.
- Added nine inherited scenario-lab scenes:
  - Movement
  - Cover
  - Overwatch
  - Doors
  - Smoke and EMP
  - Hunter AI
  - Jammer AI
  - Objectives
  - Performance stress
- Added a performance manager that samples FPS, frame time, process time, physics time, navigation time, memory, objects, draw calls and primitives.
- Added adaptive update intervals for AI, perception, visibility and tactical UI.
- Added particle and shadow budgets by quality profile.
- Added Windows, Linux and web export presets.
- Added Linux/macOS shell and Windows PowerShell scripts for testing and export.
- Added parser-based validation for environments where a Godot executable is unavailable.

## Runtime corrections made during implementation

- Developer tools now block tactical and camera input while the workbench is open.
- Dialogue and developer overlays preserve the previous tactical-pause state.
- AI target scoring was extracted into deterministic testable logic.
- Save snapshot timestamps are normalized before formatting.
- Main-menu accessibility settings are persisted through `SettingsManager`.

================================================================================
// FILE: PHASES_4_7_CHANGELOG.md
================================================================================
# Echo Godot — phases 4–7 changelog

## Phase 4: tactical command framework

- Added command states, failure reasons and metadata.
- Added attack, suppress, overwatch, interact, door, gadget, reload and wait commands.
- Added synchronized command barriers and Jammer-created release staggering.
- Added queue cancellation, removal, reordering and HUD inspection.

## Phase 5: combat and visibility

- Added aimed, burst and suppressive fire resolution.
- Added ammunition, reload timing, range falloff, cover, smoke and movement modifiers.
- Added suppression states, movement/accuracy penalties and recovery.
- Added directional overwatch and reaction shots.
- Added perception, last-known positions and aggregated player visibility.
- Added smoke and EMP deployment with tactical previews.

## Phase 6: environment

- Added reusable door states and open/close/lock/unlock/hack/breach interactions.
- Added EMP-controlled electronic locks.
- Added room-owned roof and ceiling visibility.
- Added cover, alarm and EMP-disableable electrical hazard systems.

## Phase 7: AI

- Added AI director, squad coordinator and individual decision controllers.
- Added Security Guard, Hunter and Jammer data resources.
- Added Hunter isolation/flanking logic.
- Added Jammer support, relocation and synchronization-disruption logic.
- Added an F8/F9 AI inspector.

// FILE: PHASES_8_11_CHANGELOG.md

# Echo phases 8–11 changelog

## Phase 8 — Prague vertical slice

- Added `Prague Central Hospital` mission and greybox map.
- Added network, evidence, patient, observer and extraction objective definitions.
- Added objective activation, dependencies, progress, completion and failure states.
- Added timed exclusive crisis selection and explicit crisis expiration.
- Added Observer escape AI and escape detection.
- Added mission interactables and all-living-operators extraction validation.
- Added mission result generation, score, grade, rewards and after-action scene.
- Added tactical intro, crisis and three aftermath dialogue resources.
- Fixed extraction completion ordering so successful extraction cannot be reported as failure.

## Phase 9 — Strategic layer

- Added research, facilities, construction, power capacity and persistent effects.
- Added intelligence reports and confidence aging.
- Added inventory, loadouts, equipment assignment and utility return.
- Added tactical consumption for smoke, EMP and field medkits.
- Added operator fatigue, stress, injuries, status, casualties and recovery.
- Added mission history, mission statuses, relationships, campaign threat and automatic post-mission saves.
- Added full strategic-base tabs and availability indicators.

## Phase 10 — Narrative and chapter framework

- Added persistent story conditions/actions service.
- Added chapter manager, campaign events, chapter opening dialogue and event de-duplication.
- Added conditional/timed dialogue runtime, timeout choices and campaign/tactical dialogue overlay support.
- Tactical simulation now pauses during dialogue without stopping timed choices.
- Added data-driven Prague chapter and aftermath event.
- Fixed recursive campaign mission refresh.

## Phase 11 — Asset pipeline

- Added asset manifest definition, registry and reimport-safe asset wrapper.
- Added standard character wrapper and equipment sockets.
- Expanded legacy importer for facilities, assets, nested dialogue, mission scenes, wrappers, models, portraits and icons.
- Added project validator for content, missions, dialogue, chapters and assets.
- Added asset pipeline and manifest documentation.
